# **Javify — Project Documentation** **Project Type:** Desktop Application **Technology:** Java, Swing, SQLite

## Table of Contents

1. [Description](#1-description)  
2. [Requirements](#2-requirements)  
3. [Database Tables](#3-database-tables)  
4. [ER Diagram](#4-er-diagram)  
5. [Source Code Snippets](#5-interesting-source-code-snippets)  
6. [Tests](#6-tests)  
7. [Project API](#7-project-api)

---

## 1\. Description

Javify is a local desktop music player application built with Java Swing and SQLite. It allows users to manage and play their local music library, create and organise playlists, and track their listening history in an account-based environment.

The application follows a layered architecture separating the UI, service, and data access layers. Authentication is handled securely using [BCrypt](https://www.mindrot.org/projects/jBCrypt/) password hashing. Audio playback is powered by the Java Sound API with [mp3spi](https://github.com/umjammer/mp3spi) for MP3 decoding, meaning the application does not depend on any external media runtime.

**Key features:**

* User registration and login with BCrypt password hashing  
* Auto-login on application restart via persisted session state  
* Local music library scanning (MP3, WAV, FLAC) with automatic metadata extraction via [JAudioTagger](https://www.jthink.net/jaudiotagger/)  
* Track cover, title, artist, and album display from ID3 tags  
* Searchable library with sortable columns  
* Custom playlists with cover images — create, rename, delete, and manage tracks  
* Persistent listening history per user  
* Player controls: play/pause, next/previous, seek, shuffle, repeat, volume  
* User profile management — change avatar and password  
* SVG icon support via [FlatLaf](https://www.formdev.com/flatlaf/) for cross-platform UI consistency

![][image1]

## 2\. Requirements

| Requirement | Minimum Version |
| :---- | :---- |
| Java (JDK) | 17 or higher |
| Operating System | Windows 10+, macOS 12+, Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+) |

### Build

| Tool | Version |
| :---- | :---- |
| Gradle | 8.x |
| JDK | 17+ |

### Dependencies

| Library | Purpose |
| :---- | :---- |
| sqlite-jdbc | SQLite database connectivity |
| jbcrypt | BCrypt password hashing |
| mp3spi \+ tritonus-share \+ jlayer | MP3 playback via Java Sound API |
| jflac-codec | FLAC playback support |
| jaudiotagger | ID3 metadata and artwork extraction |
| flatlaf \+ flatlaf-extras | SVG icon support |

### Running the Application

./gradlew run

Or build a self-contained JAR:

./gradlew jar

java \-jar build/libs/javify.jar

## 3\. Database Tables

The application uses a single SQLite database file located at db/javify.db. Five tables.

### Users

Stores registered user accounts. Passwords are stored as BCrypt hashes. The avatar\_data column holds the user's profile picture as a PNG.

![][image2]

![][image3]

### Tracks

Stores metadata for every audio file that has been scanned into the library. The file\_path column is unique, preventing duplicate entries for the same file. The cover\_data column holds the embedded album cover extracted from the file's ID3 tags. (genre column is not used)

![][image4]

![][image5]

### Playlists

Stores user created playlists. Each playlist belongs to a single user via the user\_id foreign key. An optional cover\_data column stores a custom cover image chosen by the user.

![][image6]

![][image7]

### Playlist tracks

A join table that maps tracks to playlists with a defined position for ordering. The composite primary key of (playlist\_id, track\_id) prevents a track from being added to the same playlist twice.

![][image8]

![][image9]

### Listening history

Records every track playback event per user. This allows the application to display a chronological listening history in the user profile.

![][image10]

![][image11]

## 4\. ER Diagram

![][image12]

**Relationships summary:**

* A user can have zero or more playlists (one-to-many)  
* A user can have zero or more entries in listening\_history (one-to-many)  
* A playlist contains zero or more tracks through playlist\_tracks (many-to-many)  
* A track can appear in zero or more playlists through playlist\_tracks (many-to-many)  
* A track can appear zero or more times in listening\_history (one-to-many)

---

## 5\. Interesting Source Code Snippets

### MP3 Decoding and Playback

Standard Java Sound cannot play MP3 files. Javify resolves this by using mp3spi to decode the compressed MP3 stream into raw PCM audio at runtime. The AudioSystem.getAudioInputStream pipeline handles any supported format transparently — WAV files pass through without conversion, while MP3 and FLAC files are decoded on the runtime.

// PlayerService.java

AudioInputStream rawStream \= AudioSystem.getAudioInputStream(new File(track.getFilePath()));

AudioFormat baseFormat \= rawStream.getFormat();

AudioInputStream playbackStream;

if (isDirectlyPlayable(baseFormat)) {

    playbackStream \= rawStream;

} else {

    AudioFormat decodedFormat \= new AudioFormat(

        AudioFormat.Encoding.PCM\_SIGNED,

        baseFormat.getSampleRate(),

        16,

        baseFormat.getChannels(),

        baseFormat.getChannels() \* 2,

        baseFormat.getSampleRate(),

        false

    );

    playbackStream \= AudioSystem.getAudioInputStream(decodedFormat, rawStream);

}

newClip \= AudioSystem.getClip();

newClip.open(in);

### Observer Pattern for Player UI Updates

Multiple UI components (PlayerBar, LibraryPanel) need to react when the player state changes. Rather than tightly coupling them, PlayerService maintains lists of listener callbacks. Any component can register itself without the service knowing anything about the UI.

// PlayerService.java — listener registration

private final List\<Consumer\<Track\>\> onTrackChangedListeners \= new ArrayList\<\>();

private final List\<Consumer\<State\>\> onStateChangedListeners \= new ArrayList\<\>();

public void setOnTrackChanged(Consumer\<Track\> trackCallback) {  
        if (trackCallback \!= null) {  
            onTrackChangedListeners.add(trackCallback);  
        }

private void notifyTrackChanged(Track track) {

    for (Consumer\<Track\> listener : new ArrayList\<\>(onTrackChangedListeners)) {

        listener.accept(track);

    }

}

// PlayerBar.java 

playerService.setOnTrackChanged(track \-\> SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() \-\> {

    titleLabel.setText(track.getTitle());

    artistLabel.setText(track.getArtist() \!= null ? track.getArtist() : "Unknown artist");

    // update cover etc.

}));

### Automatic Metadata Extraction

When a folder is scanned, JAudioTagger reads the embedded ID3 tags from each file to populate the library. Fallbacks handle files with missing or corrupt tags, using the filename as the track title rather than failing.

// LibraryService.java

private Track parseTrack(File file) {

    try {

        AudioFile audioFile \= AudioFileIO.read(file);

        Tag tag \= audioFile.getTag();

        int duration \= audioFile.getAudioHeader().getTrackLength();

        String title  \= getTag(tag, FieldKey.TITLE,  file.getName().replaceAll("\\\\.\[^.\]+$", ""));

        String artist \= getTag(tag, FieldKey.ARTIST, "Unknown Artist");

        String album  \= getTag(tag, FieldKey.ALBUM,  "Unknown Album");

        byte\[\] cover \= null;

        if (tag \!= null) {

            Artwork artwork \= tag.getFirstArtwork();

            if (artwork \!= null) cover \= artwork.getBinaryData();

        }

        return new Track(0, title, artist, album, genre, duration, file.getAbsolutePath(), cover);

    } catch (Exception e) {

        System.err.println("Failed to parse: " \+ file.getName() \+ " — " \+ e.getMessage());

        return null;

    }

}

private String getTag(Tag tag, FieldKey key, String fallback) {

    if (tag \== null) return fallback;

    try {

        String value \= tag.getFirst(key);

        return (value \!= null && \!value.isBlank()) ? value : fallback;

    } catch (Exception e) {

        return fallback;

    }

}

### Playlist Track Ordering

When a track is added to a playlist, its position is calculated automatically using a subquery, placing it at the end of the existing list. The INSERT OR IGNORE prevents the same track from being added twice.

// PlaylistDAO.java

public void addTrackToPlaylist(int playlistId, int trackId) {

    String sql \= "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO playlist\_tracks (playlist\_id, track\_id, position) " \+

        "VALUES (?, ?, (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(position), 0\) \+ 1 " \+

        "FROM playlist\_tracks WHERE playlist\_id \= ?))";

    // etc.  
}

### Persistent App State (JSON)

Rather than using a database table to save application state (last logged-in user, playback position), the program uses a small JSON file stored alongside the database. This avoids polluting the relational schema with non-relational data and survives database resets cleanly.

// AppStateDAO.java

public void savePlaybackState(int userId, Integer trackId,

                               long positionMicroseconds, PlayerService.State state) {

    StateData data \= readState();

    data.playbackByUser.put(userId, new PlaybackState(

        trackId,

        Math.max(0L, positionMicroseconds),

        state \!= null ? state : PlayerService.State.STOPPED

    ));

    writeState(data);

}

## 6\. Tests

Unit tests are written with JUnit 5 and cover the three core DAO classes. Each test class sets up its own isolated test data and cleans up after itself to avoid polluting the database between runs.

Test results are available in the HTML report: [https://reynnello.github.io/javify-tests/](https://reynnello.github.io/javify-tests/)

![][image13]

### Test Coverage Summary

| Test Class | Tests | What is covered |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| UserDAOTest | 7 | Registration, login success/failure, duplicate usernames, blank input validation, password change |
| TrackDAOTest | 6 | Track insertion, duplicate handling, search by title and artist, empty query, no-results case |
| PlaylistDAOTest | 4 | Playlist creation, retrieval by user, rename, delete |

### Running Tests

./gradlew test

HTML report generated at: build/reports/tests/test/index.html

---

## 7\. Project API

### PlayerService

The central audio engine. All UI components interact with it through callbacks rather than direct method calls from background threads.

| Method | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| setQueue(List\<Track\>, int startIndex) | Load a list of tracks and start playing from the given index |
| setQueuePausedAt(List\<Track\>, int, long) | Load tracks and seek to a position without starting playback |
| play() | Resume from paused state |
| pause() | Pause current playback |
| togglePlayPause() | Toggle between play and pause |
| next() | Skip to the next track in the queue |
| previous() | Go to previous track, or seek to start if past 3 seconds |
| seekTo(long microseconds) | Seek to an absolute position |
| setVolume(float) | Set volume 0.0–1.0 |
| toggleShuffle() | Enable or disable shuffle mode |
| toggleRepeatMode() | Enable or disable repeat mode on track |
| stop() | Stop playback and release audio resources |
| getState() | Returns current State (STOPPED, PLAYING, PAUSED) |
| getCurrentTrack() | Returns the currently loaded Track object |
| getPosition() | Current playback position in microseconds |
| getDuration() | Total duration of current track in microseconds |
| setOnTrackChanged(Consumer\<Track\>) | Register a callback fired when the track changes |
| setOnStateChanged(Consumer\<State\>) | Register a callback fired when playback state changes |
| setOnProgress(BiConsumer\<Long, Long\>) | Register a callback fired every 500ms with (position, duration) |
| setOnQueueEnd(Runnable) | Register a callback fired when the queue finishes |

### LibraryService

| Method | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| scanFolder(File) | Scan a directory for audio files and save metadata to DB |
| scanSelection(List\<File\>) | Scan a mixed list of files and directories |
| getAllTracks() | Return all tracks currently in the library |
| search(String) | Search tracks by title or artist name |

### UserDAO

| Method | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| login(String username, String password) | Verify credentials. Returns user ID or \-1 on failure |
| register(String username, String password) | Create a new account with BCrypt hash. Returns false if username taken |
| changePassword(int userId, String oldPw, String newPw) | Verify old password and update to new hash |
| updateAvatar(int userId, byte\[\]) | Save avatar image bytes to the user record |
| getAvatarData(int userId) | Retrieve avatar bytes for a user |
| getUserById(int userId) | Return a User object for auto-login on startup |

### TrackDAO

| Method | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| addTrack(Track) | Insert a track. Silently ignores duplicates by file path |
| getAllTracks() | Return all tracks, automatically pruning entries for deleted files |
| getTrackById(int) | Fetch a single track by ID |
| searchTracks(String) | Case-insensitive search on title and artist fields |

### PlaylistDAO

| Method | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| createPlaylist(String name, int userId, byte\[\] cover) | Create a new playlist for the given user |
| updatePlaylist(int id, String name, byte\[\] cover) | Rename a playlist or update its cover image |
| getPlaylistsByUser(int userId) | Return all playlists belonging to a user with their tracks |
| getTracksForPlaylist(int playlistId) | Return tracks in a playlist ordered by position |
| addTrackToPlaylist(int playlistId, int trackId) | Append a track to a playlist |
| removeTrackFromPlaylist(int playlistId, int trackId) | Remove a track from a playlist |
| deletePlaylist(int playlistId) | Delete a playlist and all its track associations |

### LisHistoryDAO

| Method | Description |
| :---- | :---- |
| addToHistory(int userId, int trackId) | Record a playback event with the current timestamp |
| getHistory(int userId) | Return the 5 most recently played tracks for a user |
| clearHistory(int userId) | Delete all listening history for a user |

---

## Database Dump

The full database schema and data export is provided in db/javify\_dump.sql  
(may look scary due to the binary data of the images)

To restore:

sqlite3 db/javify.db \< db/javify\_dump.sql

To regenerate from the running database:

sqlite3 db/javify.db .dump \> db/javify\_dump.sql
